sections.a_eyebrow: Dark Mode · Luxurious · Premium · Elegant · Refined
sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Dark Luxury — combining dark mode, luxurious, premium elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value dark mode and luxurious design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Dark Luxury style use case illustration
Dark Luxury style use case illustration
Dark Luxury style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Dark Luxury — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: dark (#0e0c08). The .inner panel has a slightly lighter background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #c9a84c as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #e8dcc8 for headings, #8a7a6a for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of dark luxury through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Cormorant Garamond — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #c9a84c. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #8a7a6a. Buttons: primary — #c9a84c background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #c9a84c border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), serif stack.
